content.problemStatement:
Write a program that checks if a given number is divisible by both 3 and 5.

[**Note:** Print the output in the same format as given below.]
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print "Divisible by both 3 and 5" if N is divisible by both 3 and 5, otherwise print "Not divisible by both 3 and 5". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. N = 15 is divisible by 3 and by 5, so the positive message is printed.
One test case with N = 20. 20 % 3 != 0, therefore it is not divisible by both 3 and 5.
One test case with N = 30. Both remainders are zero, so the positive message is printed.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Not divisible by both 3 and 5
Not divisible by both 3 and 5
Not divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5

hidden.label: tricky case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Not divisible by both 3 and 5
Not divisible by both 3 and 5
Not divisible by both 3 and 5
Divisible by both 3 and 5
Not divisible by both 3 and 5

solution.approach: The condition "divisible by both 3 and 5" is equivalent to N % 3 == 0 and N % 5 == 0. For each test case we read N and evaluate this simple Boolean expression. No extra data structures are needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
